Toggle navigation Xanthomonas euvesicatoria pv. perforans (XANTPF) Menu Overview → Distribution Host plants Categorization Reporting Photos Documents Datasheet EPPO links Overview Code created in: 2007-07-15 Basic information EPPO Code: XANTPF Preferred name: Xanthomonas euvesicatoria pv. perforans Authority: (Jones, Lacy, Bouzar, Stall & Schaad) Constantin, Cleenwerck, Maes,  Baeyen, Van Malderghem, De Vos, Cottyn Notes One of the bacteria causing bacterial spot of tomato and pepper, together with Xanthomonas euvesicatoria pv. euvesicatoria , Xanthomonas hortorum pv. gardneri , Xanthomonas vesicatoria . Other scientific names Name Authority Xanthomonas perforans Jones, Lacy, Bouzar, Stall & Schaad Common names Name Language bacterial leaf spot of pepper English bacterial scab of tomato English bacterial spot of pepper English bacterial spot of tomato English black spot of tomato English leaf spot of tomato English stem canker of tomato English Bakterielle Schwarzfleckenkrankheit: Tomate German Blattfleckenkrankheit: Tomate German gale bactérienne de la tomate French gale bactérienne du poivron French tache bactérienne du poivron French sarna bacteriana del pimiento Spanish sarna bacteriana del tomate Spanish more photos... Taxonomy Kingdom Bacteria      (
      1BACTK      ) Phylum Proteobacteria      (
      1PROBP      ) Class Gammaproteobacteria      (
      1GAMBC      ) Order Lysobacterales      (
      1LYSOO      ) Family Lysobacteraceae      (
      1LYSOF      ) Genus Xanthomonas      (
      1XANTG      ) Species Xanthomonas euvesicatoria pv. perforans      (
      XANTPF      )

seed.symptoms: Small water-soaked spots on leaves, fruit, and stems; spots turn brown-necrotic with yellow halos; scabby fruit lesions.
seed.causes: Bacterium Xanthomonas perforans; spread by rain splash, infected seed, and transplants.
seed.treatment: Copper bactericides; reduce leaf wetness; remove infected debris.
seed.prevention: Disease-free seed; crop rotation; resistant varieties; avoid overhead irrigation.
